events.text: Patient is a 62-year-old Black female with a BMI of 31.5. Family history significant for a sister diagnosed with breast cancer at age 55.

At age 55, patient diagnosed with left breast invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), upper inner quadrant (C50.2), grade 2, stage IIA (T2N0M0). ER positive (Allred 8/8), PR positive (Allred 5/8), HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 4.1), Ki-67 30%.

At age 55, left breast excisional biopsy: 2.5 cm invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2. ER positive 90%, PR positive 40%, HER2 IHC 2+, FISH amplified with HER2/CEP17 ratio 4.1. Ki-67 30%. Margins negative. 0 of 2 sentinel lymph nodes positive.
At age 55, underwent left breast lumpectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. Pathology confirmed clear margins and 0/2 nodes positive.
At age 55, received adjuvant whole breast radiation therapy, 50 Gy in 25 fractions with a 10 Gy boost to the tumor bed.
At age 55, received adjuvant TCHP (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles followed by maintenance trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
At age 56, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. Started adjuvant letrozole 2.5 mg daily planned for 10 years.
At age 55, during adjuvant TCHP developed grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, and grade 1 fatigue. Managed supportively.
At age 57, routine surveillance visit. Patient doing well on letrozole. No complaints. Physical exam unremarkable. ECOG 0.
At age 59, routine mammogram of the right breast normal. Surgical site on left with expected post-treatment changes.
At age 60, patient presented with new onset back pain and a 10-pound unintentional weight loss over 2 months.
At age 60, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed multiple new liver metastases (largest 3.5 cm), mediastinal lymphadenopathy, and lytic lesions in lumbar spine L2-L4.
At age 60, MRI brain obtained given metastatic recurrence showed no intracranial metastases.
At age 60, liver biopsy confirmed metastatic breast carcinoma, ER positive 80%, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 50%. Consistent with recurrence of known primary.
At age 60, recurrent metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer with liver, lymph node, and bone involvement. Recurrence-free interval 5 years from initial diagnosis.
At age 60, circulating tumor DNA (Guardant360) detected PIK3CA E545K mutation, TP53 R175H, ERBB2 amplification, and CCND1 amplification. TMB low.
At age 60, initiated first-line for metastatic disease: THP regimen (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days given recurrence more than 12 months after adjuvant trastuzumab.
At age 60, baseline LVEF 60% on echocardiogram.
At age 61, restaging PET/CT after 6 cycles showed partial response with significant reduction in liver lesions and decreased metabolic activity in bone lesions.
At age 61, discontinued docetaxel after 6 cycles and continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab. Resumed letrozole.
At age 61, echocardiogram showed LVEF 54%. Asymptomatic. Continued therapy with close monitoring.
At age 61, developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy from cumulative docetaxel. Persisted after discontinuation. Started duloxetine 60 mg daily.
At age 62, CT showed progressive disease with new liver lesions and enlarging bone metastases.
At age 62, transitioned to second-line T-DM1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
At age 62, patient reports increased fatigue and intermittent right upper quadrant discomfort. ECOG 1. LFTs mildly elevated. Plan: continue T-DM1 with close monitoring.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 55, left breast excisional biopsy: 2.5 cm invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2. ER positive 90%, PR positive 40%, HER2 IHC 2+, FISH amplified with HER2/CEP17 ratio 4.1. Ki-67 30%. Margins negative. 0 of 2 sentinel lymph nodes positive.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 57, routine surveillance visit. Patient doing well on letrozole. No complaints. Physical exam unremarkable. ECOG 0.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 59, routine mammogram of the right breast normal. Surgical site on left with expected post-treatment changes.

Please correlate clinically.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
